   172→ * After this call (with Signer owner), all tokens are in the hot associated token account and ready
   173→ * to use.
   174→ *
   175→ * @internal
   176→ */
   177→async function getOrCreateLightTokenAta(
   178→    rpc: Rpc,
   179→    payer: Signer,
   180→    mint: PublicKey,
   181→    owner: PublicKey | Signer,
   182→    associatedToken: PublicKey,
   183→    commitment?: Commitment,
   184→    confirmOptions?: ConfirmOptions,
   185→    wrap = false,
   186→    allowOwnerOffCurve = false,
   187→): Promise<AccountInterface> {
   188→    const ownerPubkey = getOwnerPublicKey(owner);
   189→    const ownerIsSigner = isSigner(owner);
   190→
   191→    let accountInterface: AccountInterface;
   192→    let hasHotAccount = false;
   193→
   194→    try {
   195→        // Use getAtaInterface which properly fetches by owner+mint and aggregates
   196→        // hot+cold balances. When wrap=true, also includes SPL/T22 balances.
   197→        accountInterface = await getAtaInterface(
   198→            rpc,
   199→            associatedToken,
   200→            ownerPubkey,
   201→            mint,
   202→            commitment,
   203→            LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID,
   204→            wrap,
   205→            allowOwnerOffCurve,
   206→        );
   207→
   208→        // Check if we have a hot account
   209→        hasHotAccount =
   210→            accountInterface._sources?.some(
   211→                s => s.type === TokenAccountSourceType.LightTokenHot,
   212→            ) ?? false;
   213→    } catch (error: unknown) {
   214→        if (
   215→            error instanceof TokenAccountNotFoundError ||
   216→            error instanceof TokenInvalidAccountOwnerError
   217→        ) {
   218→            // No account found (neither hot nor cold), create hot associated token account
   219→            await createLightTokenAtaIdempotent(
   220→                rpc,
   221→                payer,
   222→                mint,
   223→                ownerPubkey,
   224→                associatedToken,
   225→                confirmOptions,
   226→            );
   227→
   228→            // Fetch the newly created account
   229→            accountInterface = await getAtaInterface(
   230→                rpc,
   231→                associatedToken,
   232→                ownerPubkey,
   233→                mint,
   234→                commitment,
   235→                LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID,
   236→                wrap,
   237→                allowOwnerOffCurve,
   238→            );
   239→            hasHotAccount = true;
   240→        } else {
   241→            throw error;
   242→        }
   243→    }
   244→
   245→    // If we only have cold balance (no hot associated token account), create the hot associated token account first
   246→    if (!hasHotAccount) {
   247→        await createLightTokenAtaIdempotent(
   248→            rpc,
   249→            payer,
   250→            mint,
   251→            ownerPubkey,
   252→            associatedToken,
   253→            confirmOptions,
   254→        );
   255→    }
   256→
   257→    const ownerSigner: Signer | null = isSigner(owner)
   258→        ? owner
   259→        : payer.publicKey.equals(ownerPubkey)
   260→          ? payer
   261→          : null;
   262→
   263→    if (ownerSigner) {
   264→        const sources = accountInterface._sources ?? [];
   265→        const hasCold = sources.some(
   266→            s =>
   267→                s.type === TokenAccountSourceType.LightTokenCold &&
   268→                s.amount > BigInt(0),
   269→        );
   270→        const hasSplToWrap =
   271→            wrap &&
   272→            sources.some(
   273→                s =>
   274→                    (s.type === TokenAccountSourceType.Spl ||
   275→                        s.type === TokenAccountSourceType.Token2022) &&
   276→                    s.amount > BigInt(0),
   277→            );
   278→
   279→        if (hasCold || hasSplToWrap) {
   280→            if (
   281→                !(ownerSigner.secretKey instanceof Uint8Array) ||
   282→                ownerSigner.secretKey.length === 0
   283→            ) {
   284→                throw new Error(
   285→                    'Owner must be a valid Signer with secretKey to auto-load',
   286→                );
   287→            }
   288→
   289→            await loadAta(
   290→                rpc,
   291→                associatedToken,
   292→                ownerSigner,
   293→                mint,
   294→                payer,
   295→                confirmOptions,
   296→                undefined,
   297→                wrap,
   298→            );
   299→
   300→            // Re-fetch the updated account state
   301→            accountInterface = await getAtaInterface(
   302→                rpc,
   303→                associatedToken,
   304→                ownerPubkey,
   305→                mint,
   306→                commitment,
   307→                LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID,
   308→                wrap,
   309→                allowOwnerOffCurve,
   310→            );
   311→        }
   312→    }
   313→
   314→    const account = accountInterface.parsed;
   315→
   316→    if (!account.mint.equals(mint)) throw new TokenInvalidMintError();
   317→    if (!account.owner.equals(ownerPubkey)) throw new TokenInvalidOwnerError();
   318→
   319→    return accountInterface;
   320→}
   321→
   322→/**
   323→ * Create light-token associated token account idempotently.
   324→ * @internal
   325→ */
   326→async function createLightTokenAtaIdempotent(
   327→    rpc: Rpc,
   328→    payer: Signer,
   329→    mint: PublicKey,
   330→    owner: PublicKey,
   331→    associatedToken: PublicKey,
   332→    confirmOptions?: ConfirmOptions,
   333→): Promise<void> {
   334→    try {
   335→        const ix = createAssociatedTokenAccountInterfaceIdempotentInstruction(
   336→            payer.publicKey,
   337→            associatedToken,
   338→            owner,
   339→            mint,
   340→            LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID,
   341→        );
   342→
   343→        const { blockhash } = await rpc.getLatestBlockhash();
   344→        const tx = buildAndSignTx(
   345→            [ComputeBudgetProgram.setComputeUnitLimit({ units: 200_000 }), ix],
   346→            payer,
   347→            blockhash,
   348→            [],
   349→        );
   350→
   351→        await sendAndConfirmTx(rpc, tx, confirmOptions);
   352→    } catch {
   353→        // Ignore errors - associated token account may already exist
   354→    }
   355→}
   356→
   357→/**
   358→ * Get or create SPL/T22 associated token account.
   359→ * @internal
   360→ */
   361→async function getOrCreateSplAta(
   362→    rpc: Rpc,
   363→    payer: Signer,
   364→    mint: PublicKey,
   365→    owner: PublicKey,
   366→    associatedToken: PublicKey,
   367→    programId: PublicKey,
   368→    associatedTokenProgramId: PublicKey,
   369→    commitment?: Commitment,
   370→    confirmOptions?: ConfirmOptions,
   371→): Promise<AccountInterface> {
   372→    let accountInterface: AccountInterface;
   373→
   374→    try {
   375→        accountInterface = await getAccountInterface(
   376→            rpc,
   377→            associatedToken,
   378→            commitment,
   379→            programId,
   380→        );
   381→    } catch (error: unknown) {
   382→        // TokenAccountNotFoundError can be possible if the associated address
   383→        // has already received some lamports, becoming a system account.
   384→        if (
   385→            error instanceof TokenAccountNotFoundError ||
   386→            error instanceof TokenInvalidAccountOwnerError
   387→        ) {
   388→            // As this isn't atomic, it's possible others can create associated
   389→            // accounts meanwhile.
   390→            try {
   391→                const transaction = new Transaction().add(
   392→                    createAssociatedTokenAccountInterfaceInstruction(
   393→                        payer.publicKey,
   394→                        associatedToken,
   395→                        owner,
   396→                        mint,
   397→                        programId,
   398→                        associatedTokenProgramId,
   399→                    ),
   400→                );
   401→
   402→                await sendAndConfirmTransaction(
   403→                    rpc,
   404→                    transaction,
   405→                    [payer],
   406→                    confirmOptions,
   407→                );
   408→            } catch {
   409→                // Ignore all errors; for now there is no API-compatible way to
   410→                // selectively ignore the expected instruction error if the
   411→                // associated account exists already.
   412→            }
   413→
   414→            // Now this should always succeed
   415→            accountInterface = await getAccountInterface(
   416→                rpc,
   417→                associatedToken,
   418→                commitment,
   419→                programId,
   420→            );
   421→        } else {
   422→            throw error;
   423→        }
   424→    }
   425→
   426→    const account = accountInterface.parsed;
   427→
   428→    if (!account.mint.equals(mint)) throw new TokenInvalidMintError();
   429→    if (!account.owner.equals(owner)) throw new TokenInvalidOwnerError();
   430→
   431→    return accountInterface;
   432→}
   433→

1→import {
     2→    bn,
     3→    ParsedTokenAccount,
     4→    TreeType,
     5→    featureFlags,
     6→} from '@lightprotocol/stateless.js';
     7→
     8→import BN from 'bn.js';
     9→
    10→export const ERROR_NO_ACCOUNTS_FOUND =
    11→    'Could not find accounts to select for transfer.';
    12→
    13→export const ERROR_MIXED_TREE_TYPES =
    14→    'Cannot select accounts from different tree types (V1/V2) in the same batch. Filter accounts by tree type first.';
    15→
    16→/**
    17→ * Options for input account selection
    18→ */
    19→export interface SelectInputAccountsOptions {
    20→    /**
    21→     * Filter accounts by tree type. If provided, only accounts in trees of
    22→     * this type will be selected. This prevents mixed V1/V2 batches which
    23→     * fail at proof generation.
    24→     */
    25→    treeType?: TreeType;
    26→}
    27→
    28→/**
    29→ * Filters accounts by tree type if specified
    30→ */
    31→function filterByTreeType(
    32→    accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
    33→    treeType?: TreeType,
    34→): ParsedTokenAccount[] {
    35→    if (!treeType) return accounts;
    36→    return accounts.filter(
    37→        acc => acc.compressedAccount.treeInfo.treeType === treeType,
    38→    );
    39→}
    40→
    41→/**
    42→ * Validates that all selected accounts are from the same tree type.
    43→ * Throws if mixed tree types are detected.
    44→ * Silently skips validation if accounts don't have treeInfo (e.g. mock accounts).
    45→ */
    46→function validateSameTreeType(accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[]): void {
    47→    if (accounts.length <= 1) return;
    48→
    49→    // Skip validation if treeInfo is not available (mock accounts)
    50→    const accountsWithTreeInfo = accounts.filter(
    51→        acc => acc.compressedAccount?.treeInfo?.treeType !== undefined,
    52→    );
    53→    if (accountsWithTreeInfo.length <= 1) return;
    54→
    55→    const firstTreeType =
    56→        accountsWithTreeInfo[0].compressedAccount.treeInfo.treeType;
    57→    const hasMixedTypes = accountsWithTreeInfo.some(
    58→        acc => acc.compressedAccount.treeInfo.treeType !== firstTreeType,
    59→    );
    60→
    61→    if (hasMixedTypes) {
    62→        throw new Error(ERROR_MIXED_TREE_TYPES);
    63→    }
    64→}
    65→
    66→/**
    67→ * Groups accounts by tree type for separate processing
    68→ */
    69→export function groupAccountsByTreeType(
    70→    accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
    71→): Map<TreeType, ParsedTokenAccount[]> {
    72→    const groups = new Map<TreeType, ParsedTokenAccount[]>();
    73→
    74→    for (const account of accounts) {
    75→        const treeType = account.compressedAccount.treeInfo.treeType;
    76→        const existing = groups.get(treeType) || [];
    77→        existing.push(account);
    78→        groups.set(treeType, existing);
    79→    }
    80→
    81→    return groups;
    82→}
    83→
    84→/**
    85→ * Result of selectAccountsByPreferredTreeType
    86→ */
    87→export interface SelectedAccountsResult {
    88→    /** The selected accounts (all from the same tree type) */
    89→    accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[];
    90→    /** The tree type of the selected accounts */
    91→    treeType: TreeType;
    92→    /** Total balance of selected accounts */
    93→    totalBalance: BN;
    94→}
    95→
    96→/**
    97→ * Selects accounts by preferred tree type with automatic fallback.
    98→ *
    99→ * In V2 mode, prefers StateV2 accounts. Falls back to StateV1 if V2
   100→ * has insufficient balance.
   101→ *
   102→ * This ensures all returned accounts are from the same tree type,
   103→ * preventing mixed V1/V2 batch proof failures.
   104→ *
   105→ * @param accounts All available accounts (can be mixed V1/V2)
   106→ * @param requiredAmount Minimum amount needed (optional - if not provided, returns all from preferred type)
   107→ * @returns Selected accounts from a single tree type
   108→ */
   109→export function selectAccountsByPreferredTreeType(
   110→    accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   111→    requiredAmount?: BN,
   112→): SelectedAccountsResult {
   113→    const preferredTreeType = featureFlags.isV2()
   114→        ? TreeType.StateV2
   115→        : TreeType.StateV1;
   116→
   117→    const accountsByTreeType = groupAccountsByTreeType(accounts);
   118→
   119→    // Try preferred tree type first
   120→    let selectedTreeType = preferredTreeType;
   121→    let selectedAccounts = accountsByTreeType.get(preferredTreeType) || [];
   122→    let totalBalance = selectedAccounts.reduce(
   123→        (sum, acc) => sum.add(acc.parsed.amount),
   124→        bn(0),
   125→    );
   126→
   127→    // If insufficient balance in preferred type and requiredAmount specified, try fallback
   128→    if (requiredAmount && totalBalance.lt(requiredAmount)) {
   129→        const fallbackType =
   130→            preferredTreeType === TreeType.StateV2
   131→                ? TreeType.StateV1
   132→                : TreeType.StateV2;
   133→        const fallbackAccounts = accountsByTreeType.get(fallbackType) || [];
   134→        const fallbackBalance = fallbackAccounts.reduce(
   135→            (sum, acc) => sum.add(acc.parsed.amount),
   136→            bn(0),
   137→        );
   138→
   139→        if (fallbackBalance.gte(requiredAmount)) {
   140→            selectedTreeType = fallbackType;
   141→            selectedAccounts = fallbackAccounts;
   142→            totalBalance = fallbackBalance;
   143→        }
   144→        // If neither type has enough, proceed with preferred type
   145→        // and let downstream selection throw the insufficient balance error
   146→    }
   147→
   148→    return {
   149→        accounts: selectedAccounts,
   150→        treeType: selectedTreeType,
   151→        totalBalance,
   152→    };
   153→}
   154→
   155→/**
   156→ * Selects token accounts for approval, first trying to find an exact match, then falling back to minimum selection.
   157→ *
   158→ * @param {ParsedTokenAccount[]} accounts - Token accounts to choose from.
   159→ * @param {BN} approveAmount - Amount to approve.
   160→ * @param {number} [maxInputs=4] - Max accounts to select when falling back to minimum selection.
   161→ * @param {SelectInputAccountsOptions} [options] - Optional selection options.
   162→ * @returns {[
   163→ *   selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   164→ *   total: BN,
   165→ *   totalLamports: BN | null,
   166→ *   maxPossibleAmount: BN
   167→ * ]} - Returns:
   168→ *   - selectedAccounts: Accounts chosen for approval.
   169→ *   - total: Total amount from selected accounts.
   170→ *   - totalLamports: Total lamports from selected accounts.
   171→ *   - maxPossibleAmount: Max approvable amount given maxInputs.
   172→ */
   173→export function selectTokenAccountsForApprove(
   174→    accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   175→    approveAmount: BN,
   176→    maxInputs: number = 4,
   177→    options?: SelectInputAccountsOptions,
   178→): [
   179→    selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   180→    total: BN,
   181→    totalLamports: BN | null,
   182→    maxPossibleAmount: BN,
   183→] {
   184→    const filteredAccounts = filterByTreeType(accounts, options?.treeType);
   185→
   186→    // First try to find an exact match
   187→    const exactMatch = filteredAccounts.find(account =>
   188→        account.parsed.amount.eq(approveAmount),
   189→    );
   190→    if (exactMatch) {
   191→        return [
   192→            [exactMatch],
   193→            exactMatch.parsed.amount,
   194→            exactMatch.compressedAccount.lamports,
   195→            exactMatch.parsed.amount,
   196→        ];
   197→    }
   198→
   199→    // If no exact match, fall back to minimum selection
   200→    return selectMinCompressedTokenAccountsForTransfer(
   201→        filteredAccounts,
   202→        approveAmount,
   203→        maxInputs,
   204→        options,
   205→    );
   206→}
   207→
   208→/**
   209→ * Selects the minimum number of compressed token accounts required for a
   210→ * decompress instruction, up to a specified maximum.
   211→ *
   212→ * @param {ParsedTokenAccount[]} accounts   Token accounts to choose from.
   213→ * @param {BN} amount                       Amount to decompress.
   214→ * @param {number} [maxInputs=4]            Max accounts to select. Default
   215→ *                                          is 4.
   216→ * @param {SelectInputAccountsOptions} [options] - Optional selection options.
   217→ *
   218→ * @returns Returns selected accounts and their totals.
   219→ */
   220→export function selectMinCompressedTokenAccountsForDecompression(
   221→    accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   222→    amount: BN,
   223→    maxInputs: number = 4,
   224→    options?: SelectInputAccountsOptions,
   225→): {
   226→    selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[];
   227→    total: BN;
   228→    totalLamports: BN | null;
   229→    maxPossibleAmount: BN;
   230→} {
   231→    const [selectedAccounts, total, totalLamports, maxPossibleAmount] =
   232→        selectMinCompressedTokenAccountsForTransfer(
   233→            accounts,
   234→            amount,
   235→            maxInputs,
   236→            options,
   237→        );
   238→    return { selectedAccounts, total, totalLamports, maxPossibleAmount };
   239→}
   240→
   241→/**
   242→ * Selects the minimum number of compressed token accounts required for a
   243→ * transfer or decompression instruction, up to a specified maximum.
   244→ *
   245→ * @param {ParsedTokenAccount[]} accounts   Token accounts to choose from.
   246→ * @param {BN} transferAmount               Amount to transfer or decompress.
   247→ * @param {number} [maxInputs=4]            Max accounts to select. Default
   248→ *                                          is 4.
   249→ * @param {SelectInputAccountsOptions} [options] - Optional selection options.
   250→ *                                          Use treeType to filter by V1/V2.
   251→ *
   252→ * @returns Returns selected accounts and their totals. [
   253→ *   selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   254→ *   total: BN,
   255→ *   totalLamports: BN | null,
   256→ *   maxPossibleAmount: BN
   257→ * ]
   258→ */
   259→export function selectMinCompressedTokenAccountsForTransfer(
   260→    accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   261→    transferAmount: BN,
   262→    maxInputs: number = 4,
   263→    options?: SelectInputAccountsOptions,
   264→): [
   265→    selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   266→    total: BN,
   267→    totalLamports: BN | null,
   268→    maxPossibleAmount: BN,
   269→] {
   270→    const filteredAccounts = filterByTreeType(accounts, options?.treeType);
   271→
   272→    const [
   273→        selectedAccounts,
   274→        accumulatedAmount,
   275→        accumulatedLamports,
   276→        maxPossibleAmount,
   277→    ] = selectMinCompressedTokenAccountsForTransferOrPartial(
   278→        filteredAccounts,
   279→        transferAmount,
   280→        maxInputs,
   281→    );
   282→
   283→    // Validate selected accounts are all same tree type
   284→    validateSameTreeType(selectedAccounts);
   285→
   286→    if (accumulatedAmount.lt(bn(transferAmount))) {
   287→        const totalBalance = filteredAccounts.reduce(
   288→            (acc, account) => acc.add(account.parsed.amount),
   289→            bn(0),
   290→        );
   291→        if (selectedAccounts.length >= maxInputs) {
   292→            throw new Error(
   293→                `Account limit exceeded: max ${maxPossibleAmount.toString()} (${maxInputs} accounts) per transaction. Total balance: ${totalBalance.toString()} (${filteredAccounts.length} accounts). Consider multiple transfers to spend full balance.`,
   294→            );
   295→        } else {
   296→            throw new Error(
   297→                `Insufficient balance for transfer. Required: ${transferAmount.toString()}, available: ${totalBalance.toString()}.`,
   298→            );
   299→        }
   300→    }
   301→
   302→    if (selectedAccounts.length === 0) {
   303→        throw new Error(ERROR_NO_ACCOUNTS_FOUND);
   304→    }
   305→
   306→    return [
   307→        selectedAccounts,
   308→        accumulatedAmount,
   309→        accumulatedLamports,
   310→        maxPossibleAmount,
   311→    ];
   312→}
   313→
   314→/**
   315→ * Executes {@link selectMinCompressedTokenAccountsForTransfer} strategy,
   316→ * returns partial amounts if insufficient accounts are found instead of
   317→ * throwing an error.
   318→ */
   319→export function selectMinCompressedTokenAccountsForTransferOrPartial(
   320→    accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   321→    transferAmount: BN,
   322→    maxInputs: number = 4,
   323→): [
   324→    selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   325→    total: BN,
   326→    totalLamports: BN | null,
   327→    maxPossibleAmount: BN,
   328→] {
   329→    if (accounts.length === 0) {
   330→        throw new Error(ERROR_NO_ACCOUNTS_FOUND);
   331→    }
   332→
   333→    let accumulatedAmount = bn(0);
   334→    let accumulatedLamports = bn(0);
   335→    let maxPossibleAmount = bn(0);
   336→
   337→    const selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[] = [];
   338→
   339→    accounts.sort((a, b) => b.parsed.amount.cmp(a.parsed.amount));
   340→
   341→    for (const account of accounts) {
   342→        if (selectedAccounts.length >= maxInputs) break;
   343→        if (accumulatedAmount.gte(bn(transferAmount))) break;
   344→
   345→        if (
   346→            !account.parsed.amount.isZero() ||
   347→            !account.compressedAccount.lamports.isZero()
   348→        ) {
   349→            accumulatedAmount = accumulatedAmount.add(account.parsed.amount);
   350→            accumulatedLamports = accumulatedLamports.add(
   351→                account.compressedAccount.lamports,
   352→            );
   353→            selectedAccounts.push(account);
   354→        }
   355→    }
   356→
   357→    // Max, considering maxInputs
   358→    maxPossibleAmount = accounts
   359→        .slice(0, maxInputs)
   360→        .reduce((total, account) => total.add(account.parsed.amount), bn(0));
   361→
   362→    if (accumulatedAmount.lt(bn(transferAmount))) {
   363→        console.log(
   364→            `Insufficient balance for transfer. Requested: ${transferAmount.toString()}, Returns max available: ${maxPossibleAmount.toString()}.`,
   365→        );
   366→    }
   367→
   368→    if (selectedAccounts.length === 0) {
   369→        throw new Error(ERROR_NO_ACCOUNTS_FOUND);
   370→    }
   371→
   372→    return [
   373→        selectedAccounts,
   374→        accumulatedAmount,
   375→        accumulatedLamports,
   376→        maxPossibleAmount,
   377→    ];
   378→}
   379→
   380→/**
   381→ * Selects compressed token accounts for a transfer, ensuring one extra account
   382→ * if possible, up to maxInputs.
   383→ *
   384→ * 1. Sorts accounts by amount (desc)
   385→ * 2. Selects accounts until transfer amount is met or maxInputs is reached,
   386→ *    attempting to add one extra account if possible.
   387→ *
   388→ * @param {ParsedTokenAccount[]} accounts - The list of token accounts to select from.
   389→ * @param {BN} transferAmount - The token amount to be transferred.
   390→ * @param {number} [maxInputs=4] - The maximum number of accounts to select. Default: 4.
   391→ * @param {SelectInputAccountsOptions} [options] - Optional selection options.
   392→ *                                          Use treeType to filter by V1/V2.
   393→ * @returns {[
   394→ *   selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   395→ *   total: BN,
   396→ *   totalLamports: BN | null,
   397→ *   maxPossibleAmount: BN
   398→ * ]} - An array containing:
   399→ *   - selectedAccounts: The accounts selected for the transfer.
   400→ *   - total: The total amount accumulated from the selected accounts.
   401→ *   - totalLamports: The total lamports accumulated from the selected accounts.
   402→ *   - maxPossibleAmount: The maximum possible amount that can be transferred considering maxInputs.
   403→ *
   404→ * @example
   405→ * const accounts = [
   406→ *   { parsed: { amount: new BN(100) }, compressedAccount: { lamports: new BN(10) } },
   407→ *   { parsed: { amount: new BN(50) }, compressedAccount: { lamports: new BN(5) } },
   408→ *   { parsed: { amount: new BN(25) }, compressedAccount: { lamports: new BN(2) } },
   409→ * ];
   410→ * const transferAmount = new BN(75);
   411→ * const maxInputs = 2;
   412→ *
   413→ * const [selectedAccounts, total, totalLamports, maxPossibleAmount] =
   414→ *   selectSmartCompressedTokenAccountsForTransfer(accounts, transferAmount, maxInputs);
   415→ *
   416→ * console.log(selectedAccounts.length); // 2
   417→ * console.log(total.toString()); // '150'
   418→ * console.log(totalLamports!.toString()); // '15'
   419→ * console.log(maxPossibleAmount.toString()); // '150'
   420→ */
   421→export function selectSmartCompressedTokenAccountsForTransfer(
   422→    accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   423→    transferAmount: BN,
   424→    maxInputs: number = 4,
   425→    options?: SelectInputAccountsOptions,
   426→): [
   427→    selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   428→    total: BN,
   429→    totalLamports: BN | null,
   430→    maxPossibleAmount: BN,
   431→] {
   432→    const filteredAccounts = filterByTreeType(accounts, options?.treeType);
   433→
   434→    const [
   435→        selectedAccounts,
   436→        accumulatedAmount,
   437→        accumulatedLamports,
   438→        maxPossibleAmount,
   439→    ] = selectSmartCompressedTokenAccountsForTransferOrPartial(
   440→        filteredAccounts,
   441→        transferAmount,
   442→        maxInputs,
   443→    );
   444→
   445→    // Validate selected accounts are all same tree type
   446→    validateSameTreeType(selectedAccounts);
   447→
   448→    if (accumulatedAmount.lt(bn(transferAmount))) {
   449→        const totalBalance = filteredAccounts.reduce(
   450→            (acc, account) => acc.add(account.parsed.amount),
   451→            bn(0),
   452→        );
   453→        if (selectedAccounts.length >= maxInputs) {
   454→            throw new Error(
   455→                `Account limit exceeded: max ${maxPossibleAmount.toString()} (${maxInputs} accounts) per transaction. Total balance: ${totalBalance.toString()} (${filteredAccounts.length} accounts). Consider multiple transfers to spend full balance.`,
   456→            );
   457→        } else {
   458→            throw new Error(
   459→                `Insufficient balance. Required: ${transferAmount.toString()}, available: ${totalBalance.toString()}.`,
   460→            );
   461→        }
   462→    }
   463→
   464→    if (selectedAccounts.length === 0) {
   465→        throw new Error(ERROR_NO_ACCOUNTS_FOUND);
   466→    }
   467→
   468→    return [
   469→        selectedAccounts,
   470→        accumulatedAmount,
   471→        accumulatedLamports,
   472→        maxPossibleAmount,
   473→    ];
   474→}
   475→
   476→/**
   477→ * Executes {@link selectMinCompressedTokenAccountsForTransfer} strategy,
   478→ * returns partial amounts if insufficient accounts are found instead of
   479→ * throwing an error.
   480→ */
   481→export function selectSmartCompressedTokenAccountsForTransferOrPartial(
   482→    accounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   483→    transferAmount: BN,
   484→    maxInputs: number = 4,
   485→): [
   486→    selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[],
   487→    total: BN,
   488→    totalLamports: BN | null,
   489→    maxPossibleAmount: BN,
   490→] {
   491→    if (accounts.length === 0) {
   492→        throw new Error(ERROR_NO_ACCOUNTS_FOUND);
   493→    }
   494→
   495→    let accumulatedAmount = bn(0);
   496→    let accumulatedLamports = bn(0);
   497→
   498→    const selectedAccounts: ParsedTokenAccount[] = [];
   499→
   500→    // we can ignore zero value accounts.
   501→    const nonZeroAccounts = accounts.filter(
   502→        account =>
   503→            !account.parsed.amount.isZero() ||
   504→            !account.compressedAccount.lamports.isZero(),
   505→    );
   506→
   507→    nonZeroAccounts.sort((a, b) => b.parsed.amount.cmp(a.parsed.amount));
   508→
   509→    for (const account of nonZeroAccounts) {
   510→        if (selectedAccounts.length >= maxInputs) break;
   511→        accumulatedAmount = accumulatedAmount.add(account.parsed.amount);
   512→        accumulatedLamports = accumulatedLamports.add(
   513→            account.compressedAccount.lamports,
   514→        );
   515→        selectedAccounts.push(account);
   516→
   517→        if (accumulatedAmount.gte(bn(transferAmount))) {
   518→            // Select smallest additional account if maxInputs not reached
   519→            const remainingAccounts = nonZeroAccounts.slice(
   520→                selectedAccounts.length,
   521→            );
   522→            if (remainingAccounts.length > 0) {
   523→                const smallestAccount = remainingAccounts.reduce((min, acc) =>
   524→                    acc.parsed.amount.lt(min.parsed.amount) ? acc : min,
   525→                );
   526→                if (selectedAccounts.length < maxInputs) {
   527→                    selectedAccounts.push(smallestAccount);
   528→                    accumulatedAmount = accumulatedAmount.add(
   529→                        smallestAccount.parsed.amount,
   530→                    );
   531→                    accumulatedLamports = accumulatedLamports.add(
   532→                        smallestAccount.compressedAccount.lamports,
   533→                    );
   534→                }
   535→            }
   536→            break;
   537→        }
   538→    }
   539→
   540→    const maxPossibleAmount = nonZeroAccounts
   541→        .slice(0, maxInputs)
   542→        .reduce((max, account) => max.add(account.parsed.amount), bn(0));
   543→
   544→    if (selectedAccounts.length === 0) {
   545→        throw new Error(ERROR_NO_ACCOUNTS_FOUND);
   546→    }
   547→
   548→    return [
   549→        selectedAccounts,
   550→        accumulatedAmount,
   551→        accumulatedLamports,
   552→        maxPossibleAmount,
   553→    ];
   554→}
   555→

1→import {
     2→    ComputeBudgetProgram,
     3→    ConfirmOptions,
     4→    PublicKey,
     5→    Signer,
     6→    TransactionSignature,
     7→} from '@solana/web3.js';
     8→import {
     9→    Rpc,
    10→    dedupeSigner,
    11→    buildAndSignTx,
    12→    sendAndConfirmTx,
    13→    bn,
    14→    TreeType,
    15→} from '@lightprotocol/stateless.js';
    16→import { CompressedTokenProgram } from '../program';
    17→import { selectAccountsByPreferredTreeType } from '../utils';
    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * Max input accounts per merge.
    21→ *
    22→ * Even though V2 supports larger merges, we keep this at 4 to avoid oversized
    23→ * transactions / RPC payload limits under heavy test load.
    24→ */
    25→const MAX_MERGE_ACCOUNTS = 4;
    26→
    27→/**
    28→ * Merge multiple compressed token accounts for a given mint into fewer
    29→ * accounts. Each call merges up to 4 accounts at a time.
    30→ *
    31→ * Supports automatic V1 -> V2 migration: when running in V2 mode,
    32→ * merging V1 token accounts will produce a V2 output.
    33→ *
    34→ * IMPORTANT: Only accounts from the same tree type can be merged in one
    35→ * transaction. If you have mixed V1+V2 accounts, merge them separately.
    36→ *
    37→ * @param rpc                   RPC connection to use
    38→ * @param payer                 Fee payer
    39→ * @param mint                  SPL Mint address
    40→ * @param owner                 Owner of the token accounts to be merged
    41→ * @param confirmOptions        Options for confirming the transaction
    42→ *
    43→ * @return confirmed transaction signature
    44→ */
    45→export async function mergeTokenAccounts(
    46→    rpc: Rpc,
    47→    payer: Signer,
    48→    mint: PublicKey,
    49→    owner: Signer,
    50→    confirmOptions?: ConfirmOptions,
    51→): Promise<TransactionSignature> {
    52→    const compressedTokenAccounts = await rpc.getCompressedTokenAccountsByOwner(
    53→        owner.publicKey,
    54→        { mint },
    55→    );
    56→
    57→    if (compressedTokenAccounts.items.length === 0) {
    58→        throw new Error(
    59→            `No compressed token accounts found for mint ${mint.toBase58()}`,
    60→        );
    61→    }
    62→
    63→    if (compressedTokenAccounts.items.length === 1) {
    64→        throw new Error('Only one token account exists, nothing to merge');
    65→    }
    66→
    67→    // Select accounts from preferred tree type (V2 in V2 mode) - for merge need at least 2
    68→    const { accounts: preferredAccounts, treeType: preferredTreeType } =
    69→        selectAccountsByPreferredTreeType(compressedTokenAccounts.items);
    70→
    71→    let selectedAccounts = preferredAccounts;
    72→    let selectedTreeType = preferredTreeType;
    73→
    74→    // For merge, need at least 2 accounts of the same type
    75→    // If preferred type has < 2, try fallback type
    76→    if (selectedAccounts.length < 2) {
    77→        const fallbackType =
    78→            preferredTreeType === TreeType.StateV2
    79→                ? TreeType.StateV1
    80→                : TreeType.StateV2;
    81→        const fallbackAccounts = compressedTokenAccounts.items.filter(
    82→            acc => acc.compressedAccount.treeInfo.treeType === fallbackType,
    83→        );
    84→
    85→        if (fallbackAccounts.length >= 2) {
    86→            selectedAccounts = fallbackAccounts;
    87→            selectedTreeType = fallbackType;
    88→        } else if (
    89→            selectedAccounts.length === 1 &&
    90→            fallbackAccounts.length === 1
    91→        ) {
    92→            // Have 1 V1 and 1 V2 - can't merge mixed types
    93→            throw new Error(
    94→                'Cannot merge accounts from different tree types (V1/V2). ' +
    95→                    'You have 1 V1 and 1 V2 account - nothing to merge within same type.',
    96→            );
    97→        } else {
    98→            throw new Error(
    99→                `Not enough accounts of the same tree type to merge. ` +
   100→                    `Found: ${selectedAccounts.length} ${selectedTreeType === TreeType.StateV1 ? 'V1' : 'V2'} accounts.`,
   101→            );
   102→        }
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    // Take up to MAX_MERGE_ACCOUNTS to merge in this transaction
   106→    const batch = selectedAccounts.slice(0, MAX_MERGE_ACCOUNTS);
   107→
   108→    const proof = await rpc.getValidityProof(
   109→        batch.map(account => bn(account.compressedAccount.hash)),
   110→    );
   111→
   112→    // V1→V2 migration handled inside CompressedTokenProgram.mergeTokenAccounts
   113→    const mergeInstructions = await CompressedTokenProgram.mergeTokenAccounts({
   114→        payer: payer.publicKey,
   115→        owner: owner.publicKey,
   116→        inputCompressedTokenAccounts: batch,
   117→        mint,
   118→        recentValidityProof: proof.compressedProof,
   119→        recentInputStateRootIndices: proof.rootIndices,
   120→    });
   121→
   122→    const instructions = [
   123→        ComputeBudgetProgram.setComputeUnitLimit({ units: 1_000_000 }),
   124→        ...mergeInstructions,
   125→    ];
   126→
   127→    const { blockhash } = await rpc.getLatestBlockhash();
   128→    const additionalSigners = dedupeSigner(payer, [owner]);
   129→
   130→    const signedTx = buildAndSignTx(
   131→        instructions,
   132→        payer,
   133→        blockhash,
   134→        additionalSigners,
   135→    );
   136→
   137→    return sendAndConfirmTx(rpc, signedTx, confirmOptions);
   138→}
   139→

1→# c-Token Interface Reference
     2→
     3→Concise reference for the v3 interface surface: reads (`getAtaInterface`), loads (`loadAta`, `createLoadAtaInstructions`), and transfers (`transferInterface`, `createTransferInterfaceInstructions`).
     4→
     5→## 1. API Surface
     6→
     7→| Method                                | Path            | Purpose                                            |
     8→| ------------------------------------- | --------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
     9→| `getAtaInterface`                     | v3, unified     | Aggregate balance from hot/cold/SPL/T22 sources    |
    10→| `getOrCreateAtaInterface`             | v3              | Create ATA if missing, return interface            |
    11→| `createLoadAtaInstructions`           | v3              | Instruction batches for loading cold/wrap into ATA |
    12→| `loadAta`                             | v3              | Action: execute load, return signature             |
    13→| `createTransferInterfaceInstructions` | v3              | Instruction builder for transfers                  |
    14→| `transferInterface`                   | v3              | Action: load + transfer (destination must exist)   |
    15→| `createLightTokenTransferInstruction` | v3/instructions | Raw c-token transfer ix (no load/wrap)             |
    16→
    17→Unified (`/unified`): `wrap=true` default, aggregates SPL/T22 into c-token ATA. Standard (`v3`): `wrap=false` default.
    18→
    19→## 2. State Model (owner, mint)
    20→
    21→| Source                        | Count  | Program                |
    22→| ----------------------------- | ------ | ---------------------- |
    23→| Light Token ATA (hot)         | 0 or 1 | LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID |
    24→| Light Token compressed (cold) | 0..N   | LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID |
    25→| SPL Token ATA (hot)           | 0 or 1 | TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID       |
    26→| Token-2022 ATA (hot)          | 0 or 1 | TOKEN_2022_PROGRAM_ID  |
    27→
    28→Constraints: mint owned by one of SPL/T22 (never both). All four source types can coexist for a given (owner, mint).
    29→
    30→## 3. Modes: Unified vs Standard
    31→
    32→|              | Unified (`wrap=true`)                 | Standard (`wrap=false`, default)                         |
    33→| ------------ | ------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
    34→| Balance read | ctoken-hot + ctoken-cold + SPL + T22  | depends on `programId`                                   |
    35→| Load         | Decompress cold + Wrap SPL/T22        | Decompress cold only                                     |
    36→| Target       | c-token ATA                           | determined by `programId` / ATA type                     |
    37→| Transfer ix  | `createLightTokenTransferInstruction` | dispatched by `programId` (Light or SPL transferChecked) |
    38→
    39→### Standard mode `getAtaInterface` behavior by `programId`
    40→
    41→| `programId`              | Sources aggregated                            |
    42→| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------- |
    43→| `undefined` (default)    | ctoken-hot + ALL ctoken-cold (no SPL/T22)     |
    44→| `LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID` | ctoken-hot + ALL ctoken-cold                  |
    45→| `TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID`       | SPL hot + compressed cold (tagged `spl-cold`) |
    46→| `TOKEN_2022_PROGRAM_ID`  | T22 hot + compressed cold (tagged `t22-cold`) |
    47→
    48→Note: compressed cold accounts always have `owner = LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID` regardless of the original mint's token program. The `spl-cold` / `t22-cold` tagging is a display convention for non-unified reads.
    49→
    50→### Standard mode load behavior by ATA type
    51→
    52→| ATA type    | Target                   | Pool    |
    53→| ----------- | ------------------------ | ------- |
    54→| `ctoken`    | c-token ATA (direct)     | No pool |
    55→| `spl`       | SPL ATA (via token pool) | Yes     |
    56→| `token2022` | T22 ATA (via token pool) | Yes     |
    57→
    58→### Standard mode transfer dispatch
    59→
    60→`createTransferInterfaceInstructions` dispatches the transfer instruction based on `programId`:
    61→
    62→| `programId`              | Transfer instruction                     |
    63→| ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------- |
    64→| `LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID` | `createLightTokenTransferInstruction`    |
    65→| `TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID`       | `createTransferCheckedInstruction` (SPL) |
    66→| `TOKEN_2022_PROGRAM_ID`  | `createTransferCheckedInstruction` (T22) |
    67→
    68→For SPL/T22 with `wrap=false`: derives SPL/T22 ATAs, decompresses cold to SPL/T22 ATA via pool, then issues a standard SPL `transferChecked`. The flow is fully contained to SPL/T22 -- no Light token accounts involved.
    69→
    70→## 4. Flow Diagrams
    71→
    72→### getAtaInterface Dispatch
    73→
    74→```
    75→getAtaInterface(rpc, ata, owner, mint, commit?, programId?, wrap?)
    76→    |
    77→    +- programId=undefined (default)
    78→    |   +- wrap=true -> getUnifiedAccountInterface
    79→    |   |       -> ctoken-hot + ctoken-cold + SPL hot + T22 hot
    80→    |   +- wrap=false -> getUnifiedAccountInterface
    81→    |           -> ctoken-hot + ctoken-cold only (SPL/T22 NOT fetched)
    82→    |
    83→    +- programId=LIGHT_TOKEN -> getCTokenAccountInterface
    84→    |       -> ctoken-hot + ctoken-cold
    85→    |
    86→    +- programId=SPL|T22 -> getSplOrToken2022AccountInterface
    87→            -> SPL/T22 hot (if exists) + compressed cold (as spl-cold/t22-cold)
    88→```
    89→
    90→### Load Path (\_buildLoadBatches)
    91→
    92→```
    93→_buildLoadBatches(senderInterface, wrap, targetAta)
    94→    |
    95→    +- Filter out frozen sources (SPL/T22/cold -- cannot wrap/decompress frozen)
    96→    +- spl/t22/cold unfrozen balance = 0 -> []
    97→    |
    98→    +- wrap=true
    99→    |   +- Create c-token ATA (idempotent, if needed)
   100→    |   +- Wrap SPL (if unfrozen splBal>0)
   101→    |   +- Wrap T22 (if unfrozen t22Bal>0)
   102→    |   +- Chunk unfrozen cold by tree version (V1: {8,4,2,1}, V2: {8..1})
   103→    |
   104→    +- wrap=false
   105→    |   +- Create target ATA (ctoken/SPL/T22 per ataType, idempotent)
   106→    |   +- Chunk unfrozen cold by tree version
   107→    |
   108→    +- For each chunk: fetch proof, build decompress ix
   109→       assertUniqueInputHashes(chunks) <- hash uniqueness enforced
   110→```
   111→
   112→### Transfer Flow (createTransferInterfaceInstructions)
   113→
   114→```
   115→createTransferInterfaceInstructions(rpc, payer, mint, amount, sender, destination, options?)
   116→    |
   117→    +- amount <= 0 -> throw
   118→    +- destination: token account address (must exist; derive via getAssociatedTokenAddressInterface)
   119→    +- getAtaInterface(sender, wrap, programId)
   120→    +- hot account frozen -> throw
   121→    +- unfrozen balance < amount -> throw (reports frozen balance separately)
   122→    |
   123→    +- _buildLoadBatches(...) -> internalBatches (frozen sources excluded)
   124→    |
   125→    +- programId = SPL|T22 && !wrap -> createTransferCheckedInstruction
   126→    +- else                         -> createLightTokenTransferInstruction
   127→    |
   128→    +- Returns TransactionInstruction[][]:
   129→    +- batches.length = 0 (hot) -> [[CU, transferIx]]
   130→    +- batches.length = 1       -> [[CU, ...batch0, transferIx]]
   131→    +- batches.length > 1
   132→        -> [[CU, load0], [CU, load1], ..., [CU, ...lastBatch, transferIx]]
   133→        -> send [0..n-2] in parallel, then [n-1] after all confirm
   134→```
   135→
   136→### transferInterface (action)
   137→
   138→```
   139→transferInterface(rpc, payer, source, mint, destination, owner, amount, programId?, confirmOptions?, options?, wrap?)
   140→    |
   141→    +- Validate source == getAssociatedTokenAddressInterface(mint, owner, programId)
   142→    +- destination: token account address (must exist; derive via getAssociatedTokenAddressInterface)
   143→    +- batches = createTransferInterfaceInstructions(..., destination)
   144→    +- { rest: loads, last: transferIxs } = sliceLast(batches)
   145→    +- Send loads in parallel (if any)
   146→    +- Send transferIxs
   147→```
   148→
   149→## 5. Frozen Account Handling
   150→
   151→SPL Token behavior: `getAccount()` returns full balance + `isFrozen=true`. The on-chain program rejects `transfer` for frozen accounts. There is no client-side pre-check in `@solana/spl-token`.
   152→
   153→Light Token interface behavior:
   154→
   155→| Method                                | Frozen accounts behavior                                                                                                |
   156→| ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
   157→| `getAtaInterface`                     | Shows full balance including frozen. `_anyFrozen=true`.                                                                 |
   158→| `_buildLoadBatches`                   | Skips frozen sources (cold/SPL/T22). Only decompresses unfrozen.                                                        |
   159→| `createTransferInterfaceInstructions` | If hot account is frozen: throw. Otherwise: uses unfrozen balance only. Reports frozen amount in error if insufficient. |
   160→| `transferInterface`                   | Same as above (delegates to `createTransferInterfaceInstructions`).                                                     |
   161→
   162→Why pre-filter instead of letting on-chain fail: our multi-batch architecture means a frozen account in batch 2 of 3 would fail on-chain while batches 1 and 3 succeed, creating a messy partial-load state. Pre-filtering avoids this.
   163→
   164→## 6. Delegate Handling
   165→
   166→Compressed `TokenData` has `delegate: Option<Pubkey>` but no `delegated_amount` field. When a delegate exists, it can act on the full account amount. `convertTokenDataToAccount` sets `delegatedAmount: BigInt(0)` -- this is correct for the compressed token layout.
   167→
   168→`buildAccountInterfaceFromSources`: `_hasDelegate = sources.some(s => s.parsed.delegate !== null)`. The aggregated `parsed.delegate` comes from the primary source only (first by priority: ctoken-hot > ctoken-cold > SPL > T22). If a cold account has a delegate but the hot doesn't, `parsed.delegate` will be `null` while `_hasDelegate` is `true`.
   169→
   170→For load/transfer: `_buildLoadBatches` iterates `_sources` directly. Each cold account retains its own delegate info through the decompress instruction (`createDecompressInterfaceInstruction` includes delegate pubkeys in `packedAccountIndices`).
   171→
   172→## 7. Hash Uniqueness Guarantee
   173→
   174→Within a single call: compressed accounts fetched once globally, partitioned by tree version, each hash in exactly one batch. Enforced by `assertUniqueInputHashes`.
   175→
   176→Across concurrent calls for the same sender: not serialized. Both calls read the same hashes from `rpc.getCompressedTokenAccountsByOwner`. First tx nullifies them on-chain, second tx fails with stale hashes. This is inherent to the UTXO/nullifier model (same as Bitcoin double-spend protection). Application-level serialization required for concurrent same-sender transfers.
   177→
   178→## 8. Scenario Matrix (Unified, wrap=true)
   179→
   180→| Sender           | Recipient  | Status                            |
   181→| ---------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------- |
   182→| Hot only         | ATA exists | Works                             |
   183→| Hot only         | No ATA     | Fails (destination must exist)    |
   184→| Cold <=8         | ATA exists | Works                             |
   185→| Cold >8          | ATA exists | Works (parallel loads + transfer) |
   186→| Cold             | No ATA     | Fails (destination must exist)    |
   187→| Hot + Cold       | Any        | Works                             |
   188→| SPL hot only     | Any        | Works (wrap)                      |
   189→| SPL + Cold       | Any        | Works                             |
   190→| Hot + SPL + Cold | Any        | Works                             |
   191→| Nothing          | Any        | Throw: insufficient               |
   192→| All frozen       | Any        | Throw: frozen / insufficient      |
   193→| Partial frozen   | Any        | Works with unfrozen portion       |
   194→| amount=0         | Any        | Throw: zero amount                |
   195→| Delegated cold   | Any        | Works                             |
   196→
   197→### Standard (wrap=false) with programId
   198→
   199→| programId | Sender state | Result                                              |
   200→| --------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------- |
   201→| Light     | cold only    | Decompress to c-token ATA + Light transfer          |
   202→| Light     | hot only     | Light transfer directly                             |
   203→| Light     | hot + cold   | Decompress + Light transfer                         |
   204→| SPL       | cold only    | Create SPL ATA + decompress via pool + SPL transfer |
   205→| SPL       | hot only     | SPL transferChecked directly                        |
   206→| SPL       | hot + cold   | Decompress to SPL ATA + SPL transferChecked         |
   207→
   208→## 9. Cases NOT Covered (Audit)
   209→
   210→### Test coverage gaps
   211→
   212→| Case                                               | Status                                 |
   213→| -------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
   214→| Frozen sender (partial and full)                   | No e2e test                            |
   215→| Zero-amount transfer rejection                     | No e2e test                            |
   216→| Unified transfer (wrap=true) SPL hot-only sender   | No explicit e2e                        |
   217→| Unified transfer SPL hot + cold                    | No explicit e2e                        |
   218→| V1 tree in transfer path                           | No V1-specific test (V2 only in suite) |
   219→| Self-transfer (sender == recipient)                | No test (allowed, consolidation)       |
   220→| createTransferInterfaceInstructions with wrap=true | payment-flows uses wrap=false          |
   221→| programId=SPL, cold-only transfer                  | Tested in transfer-interface.test.ts   |
   222→| programId=SPL, hot-only transfer                   | Tested in transfer-interface.test.ts   |
   223→| programId=SPL, instruction builder                 | Tested in transfer-interface.test.ts   |
   224→
   225→### Design / out-of-scope
   226→
   227→| Case                                               | Notes                                               |
   228→| -------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
   229→| Two independent calls, same sender (e.g. two tabs) | Requires app-level locking; SDK has no shared state |
   230→

1→# Payment Integration: `createTransferInterfaceInstructions`
     2→
     3→Build transfer instructions for production payment flows. Returns
     4→`TransactionInstruction[][]` with CU budgeting, sender ATA creation,
     5→loading (decompression), and the transfer instruction. Destination token
     6→account must exist; create it via `getOrCreateAtaInterface` or
     7→`createAssociatedTokenAccountInterfaceIdempotentInstruction` before transfer.
     8→
     9→## Import
    10→
    11→```typescript
    12→// Standard (no SPL/T22 wrapping)
    13→import {
    14→    createTransferInterfaceInstructions,
    15→    getAssociatedTokenAddressInterface,
    16→    getOrCreateAtaInterface,
    17→    sliceLast,
    18→} from '@lightprotocol/compressed-token';
    19→
    20→// Unified (auto-wraps SPL/T22 to c-token ATA)
    21→import {
    22→    createTransferInterfaceInstructions,
    23→    getAssociatedTokenAddressInterface,
    24→    getOrCreateAtaInterface,
    25→    sliceLast,
    26→} from '@lightprotocol/compressed-token/unified';
    27→```
    28→
    29→## Usage
    30→
    31→```typescript
    32→// 1. Ensure destination exists, then build instruction batches
    33→const destination = getAssociatedTokenAddressInterface(
    34→    mint,
    35→    recipient.publicKey,
    36→);
    37→await getOrCreateAtaInterface(rpc, payer, mint, recipient.publicKey);
    38→
    39→const batches = await createTransferInterfaceInstructions(
    40→    rpc,
    41→    payer.publicKey,
    42→    mint,
    43→    amount,
    44→    sender.publicKey,
    45→    destination,
    46→);
    47→
    48→// 2. Customize (optional) -- append memo, priority fee, etc. to the last batch
    49→batches.at(-1)!.push(memoIx);
    50→
    51→// 3. Build all transactions
    52→const { blockhash } = await rpc.getLatestBlockhash();
    53→const txns = batches.map(ixs => buildTx(ixs, blockhash, payer));
    54→
    55→// 4. Sign all at once (one wallet prompt)
    56→const signed = await wallet.signAllTransactions(txns);
    57→
    58→// 5. Send: loads in parallel, then transfer
    59→const { rest, last } = sliceLast(signed);
    60→await Promise.all(rest.map(tx => send(tx)));
    61→await send(last);
    62→```
    63→
    64→## Return type
    65→
    66→`TransactionInstruction[][]` -- an array of transaction instruction arrays.
    67→
    68→- All batches except the last can be sent in parallel (load/decompress).
    69→- The last batch is the transfer and must be sent after all others confirm.
    70→- For a hot sender or <=8 cold inputs, the result is a single-element array.
    71→
    72→Use `sliceLast(batches)` to get `{ rest, last }` for clean send orchestration.
    73→
    74→## Options
    75→
    76→| Option      | Default                  | Description                                            |
    77→| ----------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------ |
    78→| `wrap`      | `false`                  | Include SPL/T22 wrapping to c-token ATA (unified path) |
    79→| `programId` | `LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID` | Token program ID (SPL/T22/Light)                       |
    80→
    81→## What each transaction contains
    82→
    83→| Content                     | Load transaction | Transfer transaction |
    84→| --------------------------- | :--------------: | :------------------: |
    85→| `ComputeBudgetProgram`      |       yes        |         yes          |
    86→| Sender ATA creation         | yes (idempotent) |   yes (if needed)    |
    87→| Decompress instructions     |       yes        |   yes (if needed)    |
    88→| Wrap SPL/T22 (unified only) |   first batch    |          --          |
    89→| Transfer instruction        |        --        |         yes          |
    90→
    91→## Signers
    92→
    93→All transactions require the **payer** and the **sender** as signers.
    94→

1→# Load flow audit: why it’s complex and how to align with Solana UX
     2→
     3→## Normal Solana UX (reference)
     4→
     5→- **One account:** User has an ATA (owner + mint → one address). `getAccountInfo` or SPL `getAccount()` → balance.
     6→- **One action:** Transfer = one instruction, one tx. No “load” step.
     7→- **One program:** SPL Token (or Token-2022). No “which program?” or “wrap/unwrap”.
     8→
     9→So: **one address → one balance → one tx per action.**
    10→
    11→---
    12→
    13→## Why the load flow is not that
    14→
    15→### 1. Dual state (hot vs cold)
    16→
    17→Tokens can live in two places:
    18→
    19→- **Hot:** On-chain light-token account (like a normal ATA). One `getAccountInfo` → balance.
    20→- **Cold:** Compressed (in Merkle tree). No single account; many “leaves” per owner+mint. Need indexer/RPC (`getCompressedTokenAccountsByOwner`) to list them.
    21→
    22→So “your balance” is not one fetch. It’s **hot balance + sum(cold leaves)**. The SDK hides this behind `getAtaInterface` (aggregate). That’s the first layer of complexity: unified read over multiple sources.
    23→
    24→### 2. Load-before-use
    25→
    26→To **spend** cold balance, it must become hot first (decompress). So:
    27→
    28→- **Normal Solana:** “Transfer 10” = one transfer tx.
    29→- **Compressed:** “Transfer 10” can mean: (1) load 10 from cold into hot (one or more decompress txs), (2) then transfer 10 (one tx). So “one user action” can be **N + 1 txs**.
    30→
    31→That’s the second layer: **load is a separate, multi-tx step** before the “real” action.
    32→
    33→### 3. Batch limit (8 inputs per decompress)
    34→
    35→Program limit: one decompress instruction can take at most 8 compressed inputs. So:
    36→
    37→- 20 cold leaves → 3 chunks → 3 load txs (or 3 batches in one flow).
    38→- Need chunking, proof per chunk, ordering (parallel vs sequential). That’s the third layer: **batching and proof fetching**.
    39→
    40→### 4. Three token worlds (SPL, Token-2022, light-token)
    41→
    42→We have:
    43→
    44→- SPL ATA, Token-2022 ATA, light-token ATA (three addresses for same owner+mint).
    45→- “Unified” mode: treat SPL/T22 as extra sources and **wrap** them into light-token so “one balance” includes SPL + T22 + light (hot + cold).
    46→
    47→So we get:
    48→
    49→- **programId** and **wrap** and **target ATA type** (light vs SPL vs T22).
    50→- Different transfer instructions (light-token transfer vs SPL `transferChecked`).
    51→- Decompress can target light-token ATA **or** SPL/T22 ATA (via pool). More branches in `_buildLoadBatches`.
    52→
    53→That’s the fourth layer: **multi-program and wrap/unwrap**.
    54→
    55→### 5. Options and call-site variation
    56→
    57→To support all of the above we added:
    58→
    59→- `wrap` (include SPL/T22 in balance and in load).
    60→- `programId` (which token program / which ATA we care about).
    61→- `targetAta` (where we’re loading to).
    62→- `targetAmount` (for transfer: only load what’s needed).
    63→- `interfaceOptions` (owner override, pre-fetched SPL interface info).
    64→- `ensureRecipientAta`, `sequentialLoad`, etc.
    65→
    66→So the same “transfer” or “load” has many code paths. That’s the fifth layer: **combinatorics of options**.
    67→
    68→---
    69→
    70→## Where the complexity lives (map)
    71→
    72→| Layer                    | What                                                                                                                  | Why it exists                                                      |
    73→| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
    74→| **Unified read**         | `getAtaInterface`, sources (hot/cold/SPL/T22), priority, `_sources`                                                   | One “balance” from many places.                                    |
    75→| **Load engine**          | `_buildLoadBatches`, chunking, proofs, setup vs decompress                                                            | Turn cold (and optionally SPL/T22) into hot (or into SPL/T22 ATA). |
    76→| **Instruction builders** | `createLoadAtaInstructions`, `createTransferInterfaceInstructions`, `createUnwrapInstructions`                        | Assemble 0..N load batches + final action.                         |
    77→| **Action wrappers**      | `loadAta`, `transferInterface`, `unwrap`                                                                              | Sign + send N txs (parallel or sequential).                        |
    78→| **Unified vs standard**  | unified index (`wrap=true` default) vs v3 actions (`wrap=false` default)                                              | Two APIs for “one balance” vs “one program only”.                  |
    79→| **ATA abstraction**      | `getAssociatedTokenAddressInterface`, `createAssociatedTokenAccountInterfaceIdempotentInstruction`, `checkAtaAddress` | One API for three program types.                                   |
    80→
    81→The “mess” is the **stacking** of these: dual state → load step → batching → multi-program + wrap → many options → two entry points (unified vs v3).
    82→
    83→---
    84→
    85→## How to keep it closer to Solana UX (simplify)
    86→
    87→### Principle
    88→
    89→**Default path = one mental model:** “I have a balance (we aggregate under the hood). I do an action. You send the right txs.” Minimize visible concepts (load, wrap, programId, batches) for the common case.
    90→
    91→### 1. One default “token” for apps: light-token only
    92→
    93→- **Default:** No SPL/T22 in the balance. No `wrap`. No `programId` in the API.  
    94→  So: one ATA type (light-token), one balance = hot + cold (we aggregate), one transfer instruction when we’re done loading.
    95→- **Optional:** Explicit “unified” or “wrap” mode for apps that need SPL/T22 in the same flow. Keep it behind a single opt-in (e.g. `unified: true` or `wrap: true`), not the default.
    96→
    97→Effect: Most apps see “one program, one balance, load+transfer as one operation.” Complexity stays behind one flag.
    98→
    99→### 2. Hide “load” in the action; expose “instructions” for power users
   100→
   101→- **Default:** `transferInterface(...)` means “transfer this amount”; internally we do load (if needed) + transfer. Caller doesn’t see “load” or batch count unless they inspect return or logs.
   102→- **Power users:** Keep `createTransferInterfaceInstructions` (and optionally a “plan” or batch count) for wallets that need to show “2 txs” or estimate fees. So the complexity is **available but not required**.
   103→
   104→Effect: Solana-like API: “transfer(amount)”. Multi-tx is an implementation detail unless you ask for instructions/plan.
   105→
   106→### 3. Single entry point for “balance + actions”
   107→
   108→- Prefer **one** main entry (e.g. unified or a single “light token” facade) that:
   109→    - `getBalance(owner, mint)` → aggregated (hot + cold).
   110→    - `transfer(payer, owner, mint, recipient, amount)` → does load (if needed) + transfer.
   111→    - `unwrap(...)` → load (if needed) + unwrap.
   112→- Deprecate or narrow the surface where callers must choose between “v3” and “unified” and pass `wrap`/`programId` everywhere.
   113→
   114→Effect: Fewer “modes” and fewer knobs for the common case.
   115→
   116→### 4. Reduce options in the default path
   117→
   118→- **In defaults:** No `interfaceOptions` unless delegate/owner override is needed. No `splInterfaceInfos` (fetch when needed). No `targetAmount` in the public API for transfer (we compute it from amount).
   119→- **Keep options** for: delegate, custom payer, confirm options, and one “advanced” object for the rest (wrap, programId, sequential load, etc.).
   120→
   121→Effect: Simple call sites; complexity behind one “options” bag and only when needed.
   122→
   123→### 5. Sequential load by default; parallel as opt-in
   124→
   125→- Send load batches **sequentially** by default (same ATA = same account, avoid partial load on conflict).
   126→- Optional `parallelLoad: true` for advanced use. Document that parallel is best-effort and may require retries.
   127→
   128→Effect: Predictable behavior; fewer “it sometimes failed” reports.
   129→
   130→### 6. Document the “Solana-like” contract
   131→
   132→- In docs: “Default behavior: one balance (hot + cold), one action (transfer/unwrap). We may send more than one tx under the hood. Order and count are deterministic from (owner, mint, amount).”
   133→- List the few cases where we deviate (e.g. multi-batch load, unwrap to SPL). So auditors and power users know what to expect.
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→## Summary
   138→
   139→| Why it’s complex             | Root cause                       | Simplification                                                                      |
   140→| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
   141→| Many sources for “balance”   | Hot + cold + optional SPL/T22    | Default = light-token only; aggregate hot+cold only.                                |
   142→| Load before transfer         | Compressed must decompress first | Keep load internal to transfer/unwrap; don’t expose as separate concept by default. |
   143→| Many txs for one action      | Chunking (8 inputs) + proofs     | Accept N+1 txs internally; expose “instructions” or “plan” only when needed.        |
   144→| programId / wrap / targetAta | Three programs, unified mode     | One default (light-token); wrap/unified behind one opt-in.                          |
   145→| Options explosion            | Flexibility for all combos       | Minimal defaults; one “advanced” options object.                                    |
   146→| Parallel load                | Latency vs correctness           | Sequential by default; parallel opt-in and documented.                              |
   147→
   148→---
   149→
   150→## Async calls in load / transfer / unwrap flows
   151→
   152→| Where                     | Call                                            | Purpose                                                       |
   153→| ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
   154→| **get-account-interface** | `rpc.getAccountInfo(address)`                   | Fetch hot SPL/T22/light-token account.                        |
   155→| **get-account-interface** | `rpc.getCompressedTokenAccountsByOwner(...)`    | List cold accounts for owner+mint.                            |
   156→| **get-account-interface** | `Promise.allSettled` / `Promise.all`            | Parallel hot + cold fetches.                                  |
   157→| **load-ata**              | `_getAtaInterface(...)`                         | Aggregate balance; builds source list.                        |
   158→| **load-ata**              | `_buildLoadBatches(...)`                        | Build load batches.                                           |
   159→| **\_buildLoadBatches**    | `getSplInterfaceInfos(rpc, mint)`               | SPL interface PDA + token program (wrap / decompress-to-SPL). |
   160→| **\_buildLoadBatches**    | `getMint(rpc, mint, ..., tokenProgram)`         | **Decimals only** when `options.decimals` not set.            |
   161→| **\_buildLoadBatches**    | `rpc.getValidityProofV0(proofInputs)` per chunk | ZK validity proofs per decompress chunk.                      |
   162→| **transfer-interface**    | `_getAtaInterface(...)`                         | Sender balance/sources.                                       |
   163→| **transfer-interface**    | `_buildLoadBatches(...)`                        | Load batches.                                                 |
   164→| **transfer-interface**    | `getMint` / `getMintInterface`                  | **Decimals only** when `options.decimals` not set.            |
   165→| **unwrap**                | `getSplInterfaceInfos(rpc, mint)`               | Resolve SPL interface if not passed in.                       |
   166→| **unwrap**                | `rpc.getAccountInfo(destination)`               | Ensure destination ATA exists.                                |
   167→| **unwrap**                | `_getAtaInterface(...)`                         | Source balance/sources.                                       |
   168→| **unwrap**                | `_buildLoadBatches(...)`                        | Load batches.                                                 |
   169→| **unwrap**                | `getMint(rpc, mint, ..., tokenProgram)`         | **Decimals only** when `interfaceOptions.decimals` not set.   |
   170→
   171→Passing `decimals` (e.g. `InterfaceOptions.decimals`) removes all four decimals-only RPC calls in those flows.
   172→
   173→**Target:** “As close to Solana UX as possible” = one balance, one action (transfer/unwrap), one or a few txs under the hood, with complexity opt-in and documented.
   174→

1→import {
     2→    ConfirmOptions,
     3→    PublicKey,
     4→    Signer,
     5→    TransactionSignature,
     6→    ComputeBudgetProgram,
     7→} from '@solana/web3.js';
     8→import {
     9→    sendAndConfirmTx,
    10→    buildAndSignTx,
    11→    Rpc,
    12→    dedupeSigner,
    13→    selectStateTreeInfo,
    14→    toArray,
    15→    TreeInfo,
    16→} from '@lightprotocol/stateless.js';
    17→import BN from 'bn.js';
    18→import { CompressedTokenProgram } from '../program';
    19→import {
    20→    getSplInterfaceInfos,
    21→    selectSplInterfaceInfo,
    22→    SplInterfaceInfo,
    23→} from '../utils/get-token-pool-infos';
    24→
    25→/**
    26→ * Compress SPL tokens
    27→ *
    28→ * @param rpc                   Rpc connection to use
    29→ * @param payer                 Fee payer
    30→ * @param mint                  SPL Mint address
    31→ * @param amount                Number of tokens to compress.
    32→ * @param owner                 Owner of the SPL token account.
    33→ * @param sourceTokenAccount    Source SPL token account. (ATA)
    34→ * @param toAddress             Recipient owner address.
    35→ * @param outputStateTreeInfo   Optional: State tree account that the compressed
    36→ *                              tokens should be inserted into. Defaults to a
    37→ *                              shared state tree account.
    38→ * @param splInterfaceInfo      Optional: SPL interface info.
    39→ * @param confirmOptions        Options for confirming the transaction
    40→ *
    41→ * @return Signature of the confirmed transaction
    42→ */
    43→export async function compress(
    44→    rpc: Rpc,
    45→    payer: Signer,
    46→    mint: PublicKey,
    47→    amount: number | BN | number[] | BN[],
    48→    owner: Signer,
    49→    sourceTokenAccount: PublicKey,
    50→    toAddress: PublicKey | Array<PublicKey>,
    51→    outputStateTreeInfo?: TreeInfo,
    52→    splInterfaceInfo?: SplInterfaceInfo,
    53→    confirmOptions?: ConfirmOptions,
    54→): Promise<TransactionSignature> {
    55→    outputStateTreeInfo =
    56→        outputStateTreeInfo ??
    57→        selectStateTreeInfo(await rpc.getStateTreeInfos());
    58→    splInterfaceInfo =
    59→        splInterfaceInfo ??
    60→        selectSplInterfaceInfo(await getSplInterfaceInfos(rpc, mint));
    61→
    62→    const compressIx = await CompressedTokenProgram.compress({
    63→        payer: payer.publicKey,
    64→        owner: owner.publicKey,
    65→        source: sourceTokenAccount,
    66→        toAddress,
    67→        amount,
    68→        mint,
    69→        outputStateTreeInfo,
    70→        tokenPoolInfo: splInterfaceInfo,
    71→    });
    72→
    73→    const blockhashCtx = await rpc.getLatestBlockhash();
    74→    const additionalSigners = dedupeSigner(payer, [owner]);
    75→    const signedTx = buildAndSignTx(
    76→        [
    77→            ComputeBudgetProgram.setComputeUnitLimit({
    78→                units: 130_000 + toArray(amount).length * 20_000,
    79→            }),
    80→            compressIx,
    81→        ],
    82→        payer,
    83→        blockhashCtx.blockhash,
    84→        additionalSigners,
    85→    );
    86→
    87→    return await sendAndConfirmTx(rpc, signedTx, confirmOptions, blockhashCtx);
    88→}
    89→

1→import {
     2→    ComputeBudgetProgram,
     3→    ConfirmOptions,
     4→    PublicKey,
     5→    Signer,
     6→    TransactionSignature,
     7→} from '@solana/web3.js';
     8→import {
     9→    bn,
    10→    sendAndConfirmTx,
    11→    buildAndSignTx,
    12→    Rpc,
    13→    dedupeSigner,
    14→} from '@lightprotocol/stateless.js';
    15→import BN from 'bn.js';
    16→import { CompressedTokenProgram } from '../program';
    17→import {
    18→    selectMinCompressedTokenAccountsForTransfer,
    19→    selectAccountsByPreferredTreeType,
    20→} from '../utils';
    21→import {
    22→    selectSplInterfaceInfosForDecompression,
    23→    SplInterfaceInfo,
    24→    getSplInterfaceInfos,
    25→} from '../utils/get-token-pool-infos';
    26→
    27→/**
    28→ * Decompress compressed tokens
    29→ *
    30→ * @param rpc                   Rpc connection to use
    31→ * @param payer                 Fee payer
    32→ * @param mint                  SPL Mint address
    33→ * @param amount                Number of tokens to transfer
    34→ * @param owner                 Owner of the compressed tokens
    35→ * @param toAddress             Destination **uncompressed** token account
    36→ *                              address. (ATA)
    37→ * @param splInterfaceInfos     Optional: SPL interface infos.
    38→ * @param confirmOptions        Options for confirming the transaction
    39→ *
    40→ * @return confirmed transaction signature
    41→ */
    42→export async function decompress(
    43→    rpc: Rpc,
    44→    payer: Signer,
    45→    mint: PublicKey,
    46→    amount: number | BN,
    47→    owner: Signer,
    48→    toAddress: PublicKey,
    49→    splInterfaceInfos?: SplInterfaceInfo[],
    50→    confirmOptions?: ConfirmOptions,
    51→): Promise<TransactionSignature> {
    52→    amount = bn(amount);
    53→
    54→    const compressedTokenAccounts = await rpc.getCompressedTokenAccountsByOwner(
    55→        owner.publicKey,
    56→        { mint },
    57→    );
    58→
    59→    // Select accounts from preferred tree type (V2 in V2 mode) with fallback
    60→    const { accounts: accountsToUse } = selectAccountsByPreferredTreeType(
    61→        compressedTokenAccounts.items,
    62→        amount,
    63→    );
    64→
    65→    const [inputAccounts] = selectMinCompressedTokenAccountsForTransfer(
    66→        accountsToUse,
    67→        amount,
    68→    );
    69→
    70→    const proof = await rpc.getValidityProofV0(
    71→        inputAccounts.map(account => ({
    72→            hash: account.compressedAccount.hash,
    73→            tree: account.compressedAccount.treeInfo.tree,
    74→            queue: account.compressedAccount.treeInfo.queue,
    75→        })),
    76→    );
    77→
    78→    splInterfaceInfos =
    79→        splInterfaceInfos ?? (await getSplInterfaceInfos(rpc, mint));
    80→
    81→    const selectedSplInterfaceInfos = selectSplInterfaceInfosForDecompression(
    82→        splInterfaceInfos,
    83→        amount,
    84→    );
    85→
    86→    const ix = await CompressedTokenProgram.decompress({
    87→        payer: payer.publicKey,
    88→        inputCompressedTokenAccounts: inputAccounts,
    89→        toAddress,
    90→        amount,
    91→        tokenPoolInfos: selectedSplInterfaceInfos,
    92→        recentInputStateRootIndices: proof.rootIndices,
    93→        recentValidityProof: proof.compressedProof,
    94→    });
    95→
    96→    const { blockhash } = await rpc.getLatestBlockhash();
    97→    const additionalSigners = dedupeSigner(payer, [owner]);
    98→    const signedTx = buildAndSignTx(
    99→        [ComputeBudgetProgram.setComputeUnitLimit({ units: 350_000 }), ix],
   100→        payer,
   101→        blockhash,
   102→        additionalSigners,
   103→    );
   104→    return await sendAndConfirmTx(rpc, signedTx, confirmOptions);
   105→}
   106→

1→import {
     2→    ComputeBudgetProgram,
     3→    ConfirmOptions,
     4→    PublicKey,
     5→    Signer,
     6→    TransactionSignature,
     7→} from '@solana/web3.js';
     8→import {
     9→    Rpc,
    10→    buildAndSignTx,
    11→    sendAndConfirmTx,
    12→    assertBetaEnabled,
    13→} from '@lightprotocol/stateless.js';
    14→import { createMintToInterfaceInstruction } from '../instructions/mint-to-interface';
    15→import { getMintInterface } from '../get-mint-interface';
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Mint tokens to a decompressed/onchain token account.
    19→ * Works with SPL, Token-2022, and light-token mints.
    20→ *
    21→ * This function ONLY mints to light-token associated token accounts (hot), never to compressed light-token accounts (cold).
    22→ * For light-token mints, the light mint account must exist (mint must be decompressed first).
    23→ *
    24→ * The signature matches the standard SPL mintTo for simplicity and consistency.
    25→ *
    26→ * @param rpc - RPC connection to use
    27→ * @param payer - Transaction fee payer
    28→ * @param mint - Mint address (SPL, Token-2022, or light-token mint)
    29→ * @param destination - Destination token account address (must be an existing onchain token account)
    30→ * @param authority - Mint authority (can be Signer or PublicKey if multiSigners provided)
    31→ * @param amount - Amount to mint
    32→ * @param multiSigners - Optional: Multi-signature signers (default: [])
    33→ * @param confirmOptions - Optional: Transaction confirmation options
    34→ * @param programId - Optional: Token program ID. If undefined, auto-detects.
    35→ *
    36→ * @returns Transaction signature
    37→ */
    38→export async function mintToInterface(
    39→    rpc: Rpc,
    40→    payer: Signer,
    41→    mint: PublicKey,
    42→    destination: PublicKey,
    43→    authority: Signer | PublicKey,
    44→    amount: number | bigint,
    45→    multiSigners: Signer[] = [],
    46→    confirmOptions?: ConfirmOptions,
    47→    programId?: PublicKey,
    48→): Promise<TransactionSignature> {
    49→    assertBetaEnabled();
    50→
    51→    // Fetch mint interface (auto-detects program type if not provided)
    52→    const mintInterface = await getMintInterface(
    53→        rpc,
    54→        mint,
    55→        confirmOptions?.commitment,
    56→        programId,
    57→    );
    58→
